Title: Rhaast
Age: Current3596 – 4396 years old Timeline Born3400 BN – 2600 BN Edit

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent:
When Rhaast was chosen for Ascension, his fellow Ascended, Zaahen, helped guide the ceremony. Rhaast's wit and ambition were as sharp as his scythe. Before the ritual, he gifted a beaded belt to a friend who would later become a Baccai.

After enduring the horrors of the Void and the Shuriman Empire fell, Rhaast, like the other remaining Ascended, became purposeless and their older, petty ambitions resurfaced. They taught themselves forbidden sorceries, and came to view themselves as the rightful inheritors of the world. They declared themselves Sunborn and clashed with both each other and the mortal races. Their battles brought so much devastation that the mortals eventually began to call these Sunborn Darkin, an old Shuriman word which most closely translated to The Fallen.

Rhaast eventually made his way to Ionia, along with several other Darkin, and amassed an army of followers to conquer the land. His former Ascended brother Zaahen followed them to the First Lands and aided the Kinkou Order in ending their carnage, ultimately sealing Rhaast within his scythe. During his confrontation with Zaahen, the two fought for many days and nights, even as their mortal allies rested to count their dead. Zaahen offered Rhaast a chance to fight beside him again—as he had with all his brethren he hunted—but Rhaast, like the others, refused, reveling in his newfound power. He questioned why Zaahen had not joined them, declaring that they were the new masters of this world. As they exchanged blows, Zaahen replied that they were the masters of nothing and if the Darkin could not be saved, they had to be destroyed.

Rhaast became known one as one of the five Darkin that will bring ruin to the world when they reunite, along with Aatrox, Varus, Naafiri, and an unnamed fifth Darkin.

children.title: Finding a Host
children.textContent:
Centuries later, during the Noxian invasion of Ionia, Rhaast's scythe was unearthed and was to be transfered to Noxus. Unwilling to let Noxus use a Darkin against their homeland, Zed of the Order of Shadow sent his pupil, Shieda Kayn, to intercept them. Kayn and his fellow acolyte Nakuri ambushed the Noxian caravan. One desperate Noxian attempted to wield Rhaast to defend himself, but Kayn easily dispatched him. As Kayn picked up the weapon, Rhaast whispered to him, urging him to prove himself worthy of his power. Nakuri urged Kayn to destroy the Darkin, but he refused. Kayn killed Nakuri and claimed the scythe as his own.

While Kayn saw Rhaast as a tool to help him one day lead the Order of Shadow, Rhaast saw Kayn as the ideal host to free himself from his imprisonment. Due to Kayn's strong will, Rhaast was unable to fully overtake him, leaving the two locked in a battle of wills to determine who would control whom. When Kayn is not wielding Rhaast, the Darkin's corruption recedes from his body.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent:
Rhaast's consciousness is sealed within his Darkin Scythe. Mirroring the build of Rhaast itself, the scythe seems to be made of hardened blood and steel, reflecting the body of the Darkin, contorted and reshaped by Hemomancy. It is jagged with serrated blades along the edge of it's heel, and it possesses a red eye with a slitted pupil.

When in his physical form, Rhaast is a substantially larger being, towering over most humans and other humanoid creatures. His body is primarily made of a dark red like chitin, with his arms and head made of a steel-like armor that likely is grafted to his skin. He also possesses two blade-like horns that branch out from the side of his head and curl in at the top. His eyes are a blank red and his sharp teeth are bared like a skulls'.

In this form, having taken Kayn's body, he possesses Kayn's pants and belt with minor color changes, although he seemingly uses the boots as bare legs. While wielding it in this form, the scythe's pupil also becomes jagged and the blade gains teeth.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Unlike the other Darkin, who have a clear purpose for their carnage, Rhaast takes an almost childish joy in his quest for destruction and bloodshed.

In his unleashed form, Rhaast is incredibly hammy and arrogant, maniacally laughing at his own evil deeds and appearing excited whenever he kills someone. Rhaast loves wisecracks almost as much as he hates humans, sarcastically joking that his dead victims should "walk it off" since he was hardly even trying.

Despite this, Rhaast is capable of patience, as in his scythe form, Rhaast is able to tone back his bloodlust in favor of attempting to carefully manipulate Kayn over a long period of time. But Rhaast is always ready to murder, and will gladly tell Kayn to do so.

children.title: Aatrox
children.textContent:
Being brothers in arms, Aatrox and Rhaast share common goals and similar fates tied to their origin as both Sunborn and Darkin. Having a deep kinship and mutual respect for one another, it is likely that they trust each other with little hesitation.

Though along with this fellowship, Aatrox seemingly harbors a somewhat scornful view of Rhaast, criticizing him for both his choice in weapon and failure to overtake Kayn as a host.

children.title: Varus
children.textContent:
Unlike his relationship with Aatrox, which may be tainted with a slight form of disdain, Rhaast possibly possesses a different sort of filial relationship with the Darkin archer. Having fought alongside him in both The Great Darkin War and the War of The False Kanmei, it is evident that the two Darkin are commonly seen next to one another, and likely were captured in Ionia at the same time.

Within various interactions, both Rhaast and Varus' dialogue with one another resemble quippy exchanges, with Varus casually discussing choice of vessels, and Rhaast referring to him as 'Demon Pants'.

Nevertheless, Varus, much like the other Darkin, still chastises Rhaast for his lack of impulse control, accusing him of making the other Darkin seem foolish.

children.title: Naafiri
children.textContent:
As one of his Darkin siblings, it is assumed that Rhaast and Naafiri have great history. Having been released from imprisonment since finding a host, Naafiri has now begun to search for him along with the other three Darkin that made up their primary force, looking to bring them all together to claim the world once and for all.

Naafiri remarks that she greatly approves of Rhaast's choice in vessel, but similarly to their siblings, she deems Rhaast's inability to overtake Kayn as 'unfortunate'.

children.title: Kayn
children.textContent:
In a currently fruitless internal battle with the young assassin, Rhaast's relationship with Kayn persists as both complicated and conflicted.

Despite being in a consistently aggressive fight for power, the two continue to work symbiotically in order to achieve their common goals of killing their targets, often sharing a mutual enthusiasm for the blood they spill together.

Throughout the majority of their interactions, both Rhaast and Kayn retain a level of somewhat friendly banter in the face of their obstruction of each other's desires, their sarcastic remarks rarely proceeding beyond surface-level teasing insults.

When overtaking Kayn in the conceptual future, Rhaast often mentions him in his goading, referring to him as 'the great Kayn' and consistently using his succession of him as a point of pride.

children.title: Zed
children.textContent:
Possessing a somewhat indifferent view of Zed, Rhaast still appears to hold Zed in lesser respect than he does Kayn, often mocking him and his organization.

Rhaast also comments that Zed's order 'shackles Kayn', although this may simply be a mere form of manipulation to convince Kayn to give Rhaast his body.

children.title: Skins
children.textContent: Rhaast appears in the background in the splash arts of Kayn skins.
